fields.name: Disaster  Management Cyclone.pdf
fields.summary: This document is about environmental pollution, specifically focusing on cyclones, and is part of a B.Tech course in Environmental Studies. It covers main topics such as the classification of disasters, causes and impacts of cyclones, disaster management, and cyclone mitigation, providing a comprehensive understanding of the subject. The document would be useful for students of environmental studies, disaster management professionals, and individuals interested in understanding the effects of cyclones and how to mitigate their impact. Overall, it provides a detailed overview of cyclones and their management, making it a valuable resource for those seeking to learn about this topic.

fields.name: env-book-2018.pdf
fields.summary: This document is about the core module syllabus for environmental studies for undergraduate courses in all branches of higher education. It covers topics such as the importance of environmental science, sustainable development, pollution, biodiversity, and environmental management, as well as the need for public awareness and education on environmental issues. This document would be useful for undergraduate students, educators, and policymakers in higher education institutions, particularly in India, as it provides a framework for teaching and learning about environmental studies. The syllabus aims to equip students with a clear understanding of environmental concerns and promote sustainable development practices.

fields.name: Disaster  Management Cyclone.pdf
fields.summary: This document is about environmental pollution, specifically focusing on cyclones, and is part of a B. Tech course in Environmental Studies. It covers main topics such as the classification of disasters, causes and impacts of cyclones, disaster management, and mitigation, as well as providing an overview of major cyclones in India and the country's disaster management structure. Students of environmental studies, disaster management professionals, and individuals interested in understanding the effects of cyclones and disaster mitigation would find this document useful. Overall, the document provides a comprehensive understanding of cyclones and their impact on the environment and human societies.

fields.content: Typhoons are found in the Western Pacific Ocean and north of the equator (page not specified, as page numbers are not provided in the context).
Typhoons are found in the Western Pacific Ocean and north of the equator (page not specified, as page numbers are not provided in the context).

I don't have enough information. The context does not mention the establishment year of BNHS (Bombay Natural History Society). The provided context primarily discusses disaster management, cyclone forecasting, and various organizations involved in these efforts, but it does not include information about BNHS.
The Bombay Natural History Society (BNHS) was established in 1883 (page 9).

The context does not explicitly mention typhoons, but it does discuss tropical cyclones, which are similar. According to the text (page not specified), the Indian Ocean is one of the six major cyclone-prone regions of the world. Additionally, it mentions that cyclones occur in the Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea, which are both part of the Indian Ocean. 

It's worth noting that typhoons are essentially the same as tropical cyclones or hurricanes, but the term "typhoon" is specifically used in the western Pacific Ocean. Since the context does not mention the western Pacific Ocean, but does discuss tropical cyclones in general, we can infer that typhoons would be found in similar regions, but this is not explicitly stated.
Typhoons are found in the Western Pacific Ocean and north of the equator (page not specified, as page numbers are not provided in the context).
Typhoons are found in the Western Pacific Ocean and north of the equator (page not specified).
The Bombay Natural History Society (BNHS) was established in 1883 (page not specified).
